response.justification: The candidate's profile is a poor match for a Mid-Senior C++ Capital Markets role. Their only professional C++ experience is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2015, far short of the required 5–10 years on Windows. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience, no fixed-income or investment-banking domain exposure, and the bulk of their career is in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS work. While they show strong general engineering, communication, and teaching skills, the core mandatory requirements are not met.
strengths: Strong communication background evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
Broad polyglot engineering experience across web, backend, and embedded systems
Solid software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, well below the 5–10 year requirement
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; no demonstrated Windows-platform focus
No fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience
